# RunPod CLI Dashboard

CLI tool for managing RunPod GPU pods. One command to create a pod, connect, run a service, and get a URL.

## Installation

```bash
uv add git+https://github.com/Butanium/runpod-cli-dashboard
```

This installs the `runpod-cli` command.

## First-time setup

```bash
runpod-cli config
```

Prompts for:
- Username (required) — prefixes pod names
- RunPod API key (optional if RUNPOD_API_KEY env var is set)
- Git name/email (optional, for committing on pods)
- HuggingFace token (optional, for private models)

Saved to `~/.config/runpod-cli/config.yaml`. Use `--local` to save per-project overrides to `.runpod/config.yaml`.

## Commands

```bash
runpod-cli                    # Launch pod with default settings
runpod-cli config             # Edit global config
runpod-cli config --local     # Edit local config (.runpod/config.yaml)
runpod-cli pause              # Stop pod (can resume later)
runpod-cli destroy            # Terminate and delete pod
```

## Hydra configuration

All settings are overridable on the command line using Hydra syntax:

```bash
runpod-cli gpu=a100 storage=large open_ide=false
runpod-cli --cfg job    # Print resolved config without running
```

### Config groups

**gpu=**: `a40` (default), `a100`, `h100`, `2a100`, `4a40`
**storage=**: `default` (100GB vol / 200GB disk), `large` (500GB / 500GB)
**task=**: `dashboard` (default), `testing`, `vllm`
**ide=**: `cursor` (default), `vscode`

### Top-level settings

```
cloud_type=null|SECURE|COMMUNITY   # GPU cloud type
pod_name=runpod-cli-dashboard      # Pod name suffix (prefixed with username)
target_pod_id=null                 # Use a specific existing pod
reuse=true                         # Reuse/resume latest pod if available
app_port=8501                      # Application port to expose
volume_mount_path=/workspace       # Volume mount path on the pod
ssh.username=root                  # SSH username
ssh.timeout=30                     # SSH connection timeout
stream_output=true                 # Stream tmux output to terminal
open_ide=true                      # Open IDE on pod connect
restart_command=false              # Kill existing tmux session before starting
startup_wait=360                   # Seconds to wait for pod to be ready
```

## Launching a dashboard (default)

```bash
runpod-cli
runpod-cli gpu=h100
runpod-cli gpu=a100 storage=large
```

Creates a pod, SSHes in, runs the dashboard command in tmux, opens the URL in a browser.

## Launching a vLLM server

```bash
# Basic — model is required
runpod-cli task=vllm gpu=a100 app_port=8000 task.model=meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct

# Custom LoRA settings
runpod-cli task=vllm gpu=h100 app_port=8000 \
  task.model=my-org/my-model \
  task.max_loras=8 \
  task.max_lora_rank=128 \
  task.gpu_memory_utilization=0.9

# Disable LoRA
runpod-cli task=vllm gpu=a100 app_port=8000 \
  task.model=my-org/my-model \
  task.enable_lora=false \
  task.dynamic_lora=false \
  task.docker_args='${.model} --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${app_port} --dtype ${.dtype} --enforce-eager --gpu-memory-utilization ${.gpu_memory_utilization}'
```

Uses RunPod's official vLLM template (vllm/vllm-openai:latest). The server starts as the container process — no SSH needed.

vLLM task settings:
```
task.model=???                        # REQUIRED: HuggingFace model ID
task.enable_lora=true                 # Enable LoRA adapter support
task.max_loras=4                      # Max LoRA adapters per batch
task.max_lora_rank=64                 # Max LoRA rank
task.dtype=auto                       # Model dtype
task.enforce_eager=true               # Disable CUDA graph capture
task.gpu_memory_utilization=0.95      # GPU memory fraction
task.dynamic_lora=true                # Enable runtime LoRA loading via API
```

When dynamic_lora=true, VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True is set in the container env.

### vLLM endpoints

Once running, the server exposes OpenAI-compatible endpoints at `http://<pod-ip>:<port>/v1/`:

- POST `/v1/chat/completions` — chat completions
- POST `/v1/completions` — text completions
- GET `/v1/models` — list loaded models
- POST `/v1/load_lora_adapter` — dynamically load a LoRA adapter
- POST `/v1/unload_lora_adapter` — unload a LoRA adapter
- GET `/health` — health check

## Custom configs

### Per-project task config

Create `.runpod/task/my_task.yaml` in your project:

```yaml
template_id: "runpod-torch-v240"
project_path: "/workspace/my-project"
remote_command: |
  source ~/.bashrc
  cd ${.project_path}
  git pull
  python train.py
```

Then: `runpod-cli task=my_task gpu=h100`

### Global task config

Same thing but in `~/.config/runpod-cli/configs/task/` — available from any directory.

### Custom GPU config

```yaml
# .runpod/gpu/2h100.yaml
type: "NVIDIA H100 80GB HBM3"
n: 2
```

Then: `runpod-cli gpu=2h100`

### Custom vLLM preset

```yaml
# .runpod/task/llama70b.yaml
template_id: "pvcdqlwm9r"
project_path: "/workspace"
model: "meta-llama/Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct"
enable_lora: false
dtype: bfloat16
enforce_eager: true
gpu_memory_utilization: 0.95
dynamic_lora: false
docker_args: >-
  ${.model} --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${app_port}
  --dtype ${.dtype} --enforce-eager
  --gpu-memory-utilization ${.gpu_memory_utilization}
  --tensor-parallel-size 2
```

Then: `runpod-cli task=llama70b gpu=2a100 app_port=8000`

### Config precedence

local `.runpod/` > global `~/.config/runpod-cli/configs/` > built-in package defaults.

## Pod reuse

By default (reuse=true), the CLI reuses existing pods:
1. Checks last-used pod — if running, reconnects immediately
2. If stopped and GPU matches — resumes it
3. If GPU doesn't match — searches all your pods for a compatible stopped one
4. Falls back to creating a new pod

## API key

Resolved in order: RUNPOD_API_KEY env var > config file api_key > .env file.

## SSH access

The CLI updates `~/.ssh/config` with pod connection details. After launch:

```bash
ssh <pod-name>                        # Direct SSH
cursor --remote ssh-remote+<pod-name> /workspace   # Open in IDE
```
